name: --version
options.description: Print the git version and exit

name: -C
options.description: Run as if git was started in the given path

name: -s
--short
options.description: Give output in the short format

name: -b
--branch
options.description: Show the branch and tracking info

subcommands.name: commit
subcommands.description: Record staged changes to the repository

name: -m
--message
options.description: Use the given text as the commit message

name: -a
--all
options.description: Stage all tracked, modified files before committing

name: --amend
options.description: Replace the tip of the current branch with a new commit

name: checkout
co
subcommands.description: Switch branches or restore working tree files

name: -b
options.description: Create a new branch and switch to it

args.name: branch
args.description: Branch or path to check out
